description: Symptom: Traffic is dropped for a few seconds on a MACsec channel during a VSF switchover. Scenario: When the MACsec channel has data-delay protection enabled, there can be traffic drops for a few seconds on the channel post a VSF switchover due the reset of the MKA session on the interface. Workaround: Do not use data-delay protection in a MACsec policy if the system is deployed as a VSF stack.

description: Symptom: Traffic to the CPU will be adversely limited potentially below CoPP limits when the rate-limit unknown-unicast configuration element is added at a low rate. Scenario: The switch will not receive a portion of its CPU traffic. As a result, L2 learning, ARP resolution, route learning, and multicast learning will all be impacted. Workaround: Avoid using the rate-limit unknown-multicast configuration element, or raise the rate such that it does not interfere with CPU traffic.

description: Symptom: When wireless clients roam between access points connected to the same switch, they lose connectivity and receive an APIPA address. Connectivity is only restored after the MAC-age timer expires. Scenario: When a client is authenticated with a VLAN on a secure port, the VLAN is added as an ingress-only VLAN on other secure ports, but the MSTP state for those ports remains in blocked state instead of forwarding state, preventing seamless client movement. Workaround: Reduce the MAC-age timer to minimize the impact. or configure AP ports in "device-mode" instead of "client-mode":{color:#000000}interface 1/1/X port-access client-limit 1 port-access client-mode device{color}

description: Symptom: A VSF stack member switch unexpectedly reboots due to the ops-switchd process crashing with a signal: 6 error. Scenario: In some scenarios with excessive ACL event logging, a crash may occur due to a failure in the system when processing a high volume of ACL log entries. Workaround: Minimize the use of the log keyword in ACL entries, especially on high-traffic rules. Consider using ACL counters instead of logging for monitoring traffic patterns. On the 6300 switch series, follow these additional steps: 1. After the upgrade, execute redundancy switchover (the conductor goes for a reboot and standby takes over as the new conductor). 2. After the old conductor rejoins the stack as new standby, execute redundancy switchover again.

On the 6300 switch series, follow these additional steps: 1. After the upgrade, execute redundancy switchover (the conductor goes for a reboot and standby takes over as the new conductor). 2. After the old conductor rejoins the stack as new standby, execute redundancy switchover again.

description: Symptom: Network Management Systems does not display IPv4 addresses for Aruba CX switches running AOS-CX version 10.13. In AOS-CX 10.13, neither OID returns valid IPv4 data, resulting in incomplete device visibility. Workaround: N/A.

description: Symptom: 802.1X re-authentication interval for authenticated clients does not match the configured value, with re-authentication occurring later than expected. description: Symptom: Switches may fail to respond to SNMP queries after upgrading from 10.08 version of code to 10.13 code. Scenario: This issue occurs when a switch has been configured with the "snmp-server response-source" command prior to upgrade. Workaround: After upgrading, access the switch shell and either: 1. Delete the file/fs/nos/net-snmp/agent-swns/snmp.conf 2. Edit the file to comment out (with #) the line containing[snmp] srcipaddrentry 3. Restart the SNMP service with systemctl restart snmpd-wrapper-in-c.service.

description: Symptom: In an L2VPN network, when a DHCP client sends an IP request with the broadcast flag unset (unicast), duplicate DHCP client packets are observed at the DHCP server-one from each VTEP. As a result, the client does not receive an IP address. Scenario: To prevent duplicate DHCP client packets from each VTEP in an L2VPN topology, it is recommended to configure "no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients" on the switch. However, after saving the switch configuration and rebooting, the relay daemon does not correctly apply all configuration settings at boot time, even though the configuration persists in the database. This causes the duplicate packet issue to reappear. Workaround: To restore correct behavior, manually enable and then disable the L2VPN client configuration using "dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients" followed by "no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients".

description: Symptom: Continuous system reboots occur on the affected switch. The system enters a reboot loop, preventing normal network operation and management access. Scenario: This issue occurs in a specific VSF environment when the VXLAN Tunnel Underlay routes are being resolved. Before the system could process the route resolution and program the tunnel, the commander went for a reboot. When the new commander came and when the system started processing the request, it programmed tunnel underlay with invalid information. The invalid configuration prevents the member from successfully joining the stack, triggering continuous reboots. Workaround: To resolve this issue, flap the underlay connections using the VXLAN shut and no shut commands. These events will clear the incorrect status information and should reprogram the tunnel properly.

description: Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up. Scenario: This issue can occur while changing port speeds to 2.5G on a 6300 switch using gPTP. Workaround: Use 1G/5G/10G port speeds instead.

description: Symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails. Scenario: Downloading/uploading the software Image via sm ubuntu IPv6 TFTP server fails. Workaround: Use the blocksize option in the copy command with a blocksize of 1375 or less. For example: Copy copy tftp://[20:1::100];blocksize=1375/image.swi secondary vrf vrf1

description: Symptom: Traffic is dropped for a few seconds on a MACsec channel during a VSF switchover. Scenario: When the MACsec channel has data-delay protection enabled, there can be traffic drops for a few seconds on the channel post a VSF switchover due the reset of the MKA session on the interface. Workaround: Do not use data-delay protection in a MACsec policy if the system is deployed as a VSF stack.
